changes_made: Renamed k8s_rbac_user_access_review to k8s_rbac_user_access_review_conducted for clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes RBAC controls relevant to workforce clearance procedures under HIPAA. The naming convention is mostly followed, but 'k8s_rbac_user_access_review' could be more descriptive to indicate the action of conducting a review.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'conducted' in 'k8s_rbac_user_access_review' to clearly indicate the action being checked.
title: 164_308_a_3_ii_b: 164.308(a)(3)(ii)(B) Workforce clearance procedure
step1_reasoning: The HIPAA control 164.308(a)(3)(ii)(B) focuses on workforce clearance procedures, which can be mapped to ensuring that Kubernetes RBAC configurations enforce least privilege and prevent excessive permissions. This includes ensuring no cluster-wide administrative privileges are granted unnecessarily, enforcing least privilege principles, and reviewing user access. Additionally, disabling automatic mounting of service account tokens helps prevent unauthorized access.
